title: Connect the Anydocs MCP Server to Codex
description: Give Codex a safe way to read and write Anydocs projects.

render.markdown:
# Connect the Anydocs MCP Server to Codex

A practical setup looks like this:

1. Register `@anydocs/mcp` in Codex as a `stdio` MCP server
2. Make sure Codex reads the project's `AGENTS.md`
3. Default to this workflow:
   - `project_open`
   - `project_validate` when project state is uncertain
   - `page_list` / `page_find` / `page_get`
   - `page_create` / `page_update` / `page_set_status`
   - `nav_get`

This keeps page mutations inside Anydocs domain validation, preserves publish rules, and makes agent behavior much more predictable.
render.plainText: Connect the Anydocs MCP server to Codex by registering @anydocs/mcp as a stdio server and letting Codex read the project's AGENTS.md. Then use project_open, validation, page reads, page writes, and nav_get as the default workflow.
